rulings.Case Title: JESSIE MENDOZA et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, DECEMBER 10, 2007, LINE 1, DEFENDANT NEDLLOYD LINES (USA) CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO CLARIFY THE EMPLOYMENT STATUS OF RICHARD MENDOZA. GIVEN THE UNIQUE STATUTES AT ISSUE, CLARIFICATION IS WARRENTED. AS TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING A CLAIM OF LIABILITY ON BEHALF OF A SHORE-BASED WORKER. IN THE 7TH THROUGHT THE 9TH CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST CLARIFY MR. MENDOZA'S EMPLOYMENT STATUS. GIVEN THAT PLAINTIFF'S CAUSE OF ACTION DOES NOT ARISE UNTIL THE INJURY IS MANIFESTED, IT WOULD APPEAR THERE IS NO BASIS TO AMEND THE 9TH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE 10TH AND 11TH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE PRESUMED ON THE PRECEEDING CAUSES OF ACTION 6-9. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: JANET PICKENS VS. DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES - CCSF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Motion And Motion To Require Pltf To Furnish Security And To Enter Pre-Filing Order Pursuant To Ccp 391.7
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, DECEMBER 10, 2007, LINE 12, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Re-Notice Of Motion And Motion To Require Plantiff To Furnish Security And To Enter Pre-Filing Order Pursuant To Ccp 391.7. RENOTICED FROM 12/3/07. GRANTED; $1,000 BOND REQUIRED. =(302/PJM) SEE MINUTES BELOW OF DEC. 3, 2007: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Require Plaintiff To Furnish Security Pursuant To CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 391.1 And To Enter Pre Filing Order (Request For Judicial Notice) IS GRANTED. THE COURT DECLARES PLAINTIFF TO BE A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 391(B)(1), (B)(2) AND (B)(3). THERE IS NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT PLAINTIFF WILL PREVAIL IN THIS LITIGATION. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO FURNISH SECURITY WITH THE COURT IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,000.00 ON OR BEFORE DECEMBER 15, 2007 OR THIS ACTION WILL BE DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE. THE COURT ENTERS A PRE-FILING ORDER PROHIBITING PLAINTIFF FROM FILING ANY NEW LITIGATION IN PROPERIA PERSONA WITHOUT FIRST OBTAINING LEAVE OF THE PRESIDING JUDGE. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600 (302/PJM)
